steps.name: Connector adapters (arXiv, HN-Algolia, GitHub, RSS-list, SearXNG)
Node orchestration - connector-failure tolerance (BR-1) + SSE progress
Wire remaining tests for all ACs

decisions: Connectors are complementary sources queried on every run (BR-1), not chain-failover alternatives -- collect_sources() calls each registered search adapter directly via the registry rather than through ProviderRouter.call() (which implements single-chain-walk semantics that don't fit here). See patterns/langgraph-pipeline-node.md.

RSS connector has no server-side search endpoint -- fetches the 6 configured trusted vendor-blog feeds and filters client-side by a title/summary substring match against the query; feed list overridable via ProviderSettings.extra['rss_feeds'] (comma-separated), defaulting to the 'Decisions already locked' 6-feed list.

Paywall detection is a length heuristic (<400 extracted chars via trafilatura -> partial_content=true) rather than domain-specific paywall-selector rules -- simple, good enough for the stated Scope, revisit only if false-positive rate becomes a real problem.

Embedding-similarity dedup (BR-2/AC5) is fully implemented and unit-tested in isolation (dedupe.py), but the wired end-to-end node (run.py/node.py) only exercises url_hash-based dedup for now, since real BGE-M3 embedding calls aren't wired into the research flow yet -- AC1's fixture (12 articles, 2 exact-URL duplicates, 1 paywall -> 10 sources) doesn't require embedding similarity to pass, and doing real embedding calls per source would need a live LLM/embedding provider not available in this sandbox.

IMPORTANT/unresolved: app/pipeline/nodes/research/node.py::research_node (the real, DB/router-touching implementation) is NOT wired into app/pipeline/graph.py's live NODE_FNS[NodeName.RESEARCH] -- doing so would break every one of 4-1's happy-path tests, which build a real compiled graph and run the RESEARCH stub with no DB mocking. The real function is complete and independently tested (mocking Database/ProviderRouter at its import points); actually swapping it in requires either a live-DB test environment or a structural change to how nodes receive session/router. Flagged in detail in patterns/langgraph-pipeline-node.md for 4-4/4-5/4-6/4-7 to read before hitting the same wall.

Force-pushed over a stale origin/feat/4-3-node-research branch (based on a commit far behind current main, bundling an abandoned parallel 4-2 attempt already superseded by the real 4-2 merged to main) -- same pattern as 4-1's stale branch, documented in project-memory.md.
